Issue: Veeam Agent for Microsoft Windows was unable to serialize the local disks and shadow volumes, showing an error: "Failed to serialize listing of the local hard disks and shadow volumes. Cannot create XML document. COM error: Code: 0x80040154."


Actions Taken:


Verified and restarted the Volume Shadow Copy and Microsoft Software Shadow Copy Provider services.


Ran the vssadmin list writers command, encountering the error: "A Volume Shadow Copy Service component encountered an unexpected error."


Attempted to re-register the swprv.dll component using the regsvr32 command, but received the error: "The module 'swprv.dll' was loaded but the call to DllRegisterServer failed with error code 0x80070715."


Ran System File Checker (sfc /scannow) to check for any system file corruption.


Checked Event Viewer for VSS-related errors and reviewed logs for further analysis.


Repaired the Veeam Agent installation and attempted manual license installation, encountering the "External component has thrown an exception" error.


Performed a full disk check (chkdsk /f) and verified disk integrity.


Attempted multiple reinstallation and re-registration steps for Veeam and VSS components.


Current Status: Issue remains unresolved at this point. Further investigation required with Veeam support or possible deeper system-level troubleshooting.

Title: Unusual repo space showing for Veeam repo
Notes: As per monitoring alerts, it was observed that the Veeam repository for ANI was reporting unusually high used space exceeding its actual capacity. To investigate further, I connected to the Veeam Backup & Replication (VBR) server and accessed the QNAP repository, where multiple old snapshots were identified consuming excessive space. The older and unnecessary snapshots were deleted successfully, after which a repository rescan was performed from the VBR console. Post-rescan, the repository usage was corrected, and the used space was reduced from approximately 304 TB to 174 TB, confirming that the issue is related to the snapshots on the qnap.

Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - ANI-HQ-VONE: (10.5.5.89) - Disk Utilization is 68%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
Notes: As per the alert connected to ANI-HQ-VONE, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 55% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.
